slides.title: 1. The Universal Set (ξ)
slides.text:
Imagine a big box that contains everything we are talking about. This is the Universal Set, marked with the symbol ξ.

Here, ξ = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8}

slides.title: 2. Intersection (∩)
slides.text:
The Intersection is where the two circles overlap. These are numbers that are in Set A AND Set B.

A ∩ B = { 3, 4 }

slides.title: 3. Union (∪)
slides.text:
The Union brings everyone together! It includes Set A, Set B, and the middle.

It is the marriage of the two sets.
A ∪ B = { 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 }

slides.title: 4. Difference (A - B)
slides.text:
Set Difference means 'A Only'. We start with Set A, but we remove anything that touches B.

A - B = { 1, 2 }
(Notice 3 and 4 are gone because they are in B)

slides.title: 5. Complement of A (A')
slides.text:
Complement means 'Everything outside of A'. Cover circle A with your hand. What is left in the Universal box?

A' = { 5, 6, 7, 8 }
